data.text: I'll start by reading the current CI workflow and relevant files before making changes.

data.output:
1	name: CI
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [ main, develop ]
6	  pull_request:
7	    branches: [ main ]
8	  schedule:
9	    - cron: '0 4 * * 1'  # Weekly on Monday at 04:00 UTC
10	  workflow_dispatch:
11	
12	# Minimal permissions by default
13	permissions:
14	  contents: read
15	  checks: write
16	  pull-requests: write
17	
18	jobs:
19	  # Main CI job using lefthook
20	  ci:
21	    name: CI Validation
22	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
23	    steps:
24	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
25	      with:
26	        fetch-depth: 0  # Need full history for some checks
27	    
28	    - name: Set up Go
29	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
30	      with:
31	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
32	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
33	
34	    - name: golangci-lint
35	      uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v8
36	      with:
37	        version: v2.11.4
38	        working-directory: cli
39	        skip-cache: false
40	
41	    - name: Install lefthook
42	      run: |
43	        # Install using Go for better reliability in CI
44	        go install github.com/evilmartians/lefthook@latest
45	        echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
46	
47	    - name: Verify git-subtree availability
48	      run: |
49	        # git-subtree should be included with git, verify it's available
50	        if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
51	          echo "git-subtree not found, attempting to install..."
52	          # On some distros it's a separate package
53	          sudo apt-get update
54	          sudo apt-get install -y git-subtree || true
55	          # Verify again
56	          if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
57	            echo "ERROR: git-subtree is not available"
58	            exit 1
59	          fi
60	        fi
61	        echo "✓ git-subtree is available"
62	
63	    - name: Set up Bun
64	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
65	      with:
66	        bun-version: latest
67	
68	    - name: Install root dependencies
69	      run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
70	
71	    - name: Lint DESIGN.md
72	      run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
73	
74	    - name: Build CLI binary
75	      run: |
76	        cd cli
77	        make build
78	
79	    - name: Validate bead schema
80	      run: make bead-schema
81	
82	    - name: Validate skill metadata
83	      run: make skill-schema
84	
85	    - name: Validate ddx skill (agentskills.io conformance)
86	      run: scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate
87	
88	    - name: Run CI validation
89	      run: lefthook run ci
90	      env:
91	        LEFTHOOK_QUIET: meta,execution
92	
93	    - name: Guard server state against test pollution
94	      run: |
95	        set -euo pipefail
96	
97	        export XDG_DATA_HOME="$(mktemp -d)"
98	
99	        cd cli
100	        go test ./... -count=1
101	
102	        ./build/ddx server --addr 127.0.0.1 --port 17743 --tsnet=false > ../ddx-state-guard-server.log 2>&1 &
103	        server_pid=$!
104	        cleanup() {
105	          kill "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
106	          wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
107	        }
108	        trap cleanup EXIT
109	
110	        state_file="$XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/server-state.json"
111	        for _ in $(seq 1 20); do
112	          if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
113	            break
114	          fi
115	          sleep 0.25
116	        done
117	
118	        if [ ! -f "$state_file" ]; then
119	          cat ../ddx-state-guard-server.log
120	          echo "server-state.json was not created"
121	          exit 1
122	        fi
123	
124	        project_count="$(jq '.projects | length' "$state_file")"
125	        if [ "$project_count" -gt 20 ]; then
126	          jq '.projects[] | {name, path}' "$state_file"
127	          echo "server-state.json contains $project_count projects; expected <= 20"
128	          exit 1
129	        fi
130	
131	        if jq -e '.projects[]? | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)"))' "$state_file" >/dev/null; then
132	          jq '.projects[] | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)")) | {name, path}' "$state_file"
133	          echo "server-state.json contains Go test temp-dir project entries"
134	          exit 1
135	        fi
136	
137	  # Separate build job for matrix builds
138	  build:
139	    name: Build
140	    needs: ci
141	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
142	    strategy:
143	      matrix:
144	        os: [linux, darwin, windows]
145	        arch: [amd64, arm64]
146	        exclude:
147	          - os: windows
148	            arch: arm64
149	    defaults:
150	      run:
151	        working-directory: cli
152	    steps:
153	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
154	    
155	    - name: Set up Go
156	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
157	      with:
158	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
159	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
160	    
161	    - name: Build binary
162	      run: |
163	        GOOS=${{ matrix.os }} GOARCH=${{ matrix.arch }} \
164	          go build -v -o ../bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }} .
165	    
166	    - name: Upload artifacts
167	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
168	      with:
169	        name: ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
170	        path: bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
171	        retention-days: 7
172	
173	  # Integration tests
174	  integration:
175	    name: Integration Tests
176	    needs: ci
177	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
178	    defaults:
179	      run:
180	        working-directory: cli
181	    steps:
182	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
183	
184	    - name: Set up Go
185	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
186	      with:
187	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
188	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
189	
190	    - name: Build binary
191	      run: go build -o ../bin/ddx .
192	
193	    - name: Run E2E smoke tests
194	      run: go test -v ./cmd -run TestE2ESmokeJourney -count=1 2>&1 | tee ../e2e-test-output.txt
195	
196	    - name: Upload E2E test output
197	      if: always()
198	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
199	      with:
200	        name: e2e-test-output
201	        path: e2e-test-output.txt
202	        retention-days: 7
203	
204	    - name: Render execute-bead prompt-size report
205	      env:
206	        DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT: ${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report.txt
207	      run: go test -v ./internal/agent -run '^TestPromptSizeReport$' -count=1 2>&1 | tee ../prompt-size-report.log
208	
209	    - name: Upload prompt-size report
210	      if: always()
211	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
212	      with:
213	        name: prompt-size-report
214	        path: |
215	          prompt-size-report.txt
216	          prompt-size-report.json
217	          prompt-size-report.log
218	        retention-days: 14
219	
220	  frontend-e2e:
221	    name: Frontend Playwright E2E
222	    needs: ci
223	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
224	    steps:
225	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
226	
227	    - name: Set up Go
228	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
229	      with:
230	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
231	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
232	
233	    - name: Set up Bun
234	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
235	      with:
236	        bun-version: latest
237	
238	    - name: Install frontend dependencies
239	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
240	      run: bun install
241	
242	    - name: Build frontend bundle
243	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
244	      run: bun run build
245	
246	    - name: Build CLI binary
247	      working-directory: cli
248	      run: make build
249	
250	    - name: Install Playwright browsers
251	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
252	      run: bunx playwright install --with-deps chromium
253	
254	    - name: Run frontend unit tests
255	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
256	      run: bun run test
257	
258	    - name: Run frontend Playwright E2E tests
259	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
260	      run: bun run test:e2e
261	
262	  # Perf tests — non-gating; run weekly on schedule or on manual dispatch only
263	  perf:
264	    name: Perf Tests
265	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
266	    if: github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
267	    defaults:
268	      run:
269	        working-directory: cli
270	    steps:
271	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
272	
273	    - name: Set up Go
274	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
275	      with:
276	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
277	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
278	
279	    - name: Run perf tests
280	      run: make test-perf

data.text: Let me read CLAUDE.md to find the right location for documentation:

data.output:
160	The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:
161	
162	**Core Commands:**
163	- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
164	- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
165	- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
166	- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
167	- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
168	- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status
169	
170	**Bead Tracker:**
171	- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
172	- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
173	- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
174	- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br
175	- `ddx bead migrate` - Externalize closed-bead events to `.ddx/attachments/` and move eligible closed beads into `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`. Idempotent; safe to re-run.
176	
177	**Queue Work:**
178	- `ddx work` - Drain the bead execution queue (alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`; all flags pass through)
179	
180	**Agent Service:**
181	- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
182	- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
183	- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
184	- `ddx agent execute-loop` - Drain bead queue (prefer `ddx work`)
185	- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
186	- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
187	- `ddx agent log` - Session history
188	
189	**Resource Commands:**
190	- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
191	- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
192	- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
193	- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers
194	
195	**Embedded Utilities:**
196	- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required
197	
198	## Persona System
199	
200	DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:
201	
202	- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`, `architect`)
203	- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
204	- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`
205	
206	Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.
207	
208	## When filing beads
209	
210	Bead descriptions + AC must be sufficient context for a sub-agent to execute the work without operator hand-curation (principle P7, `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`). Before invoking `ddx bead create`, walk the 8-criterion rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`: title clarity, root cause with file:line, numbered AC with specific test names, wired-in assertion, `cd cli && go test` + lefthook gate, labels with cross-refs, parent + deps, standalone description (no `/tmp/*`, no chat references). Beads scoring < 7/8 will fail in autonomous drain.
211	
212	See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
213	
214	## Execution Evidence Convention
215	
216	When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.
217	
218	## When filing beads
219	
220	A bead's body is the entire prompt the executing sub-agent will see — there is no chat history, no operator hand-curation, no out-of-band context. Every bead must therefore satisfy the 8-criterion rubric documented in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` before it is filed or dispatched.
221	
222	In short:
223	
224	- **Title**: imperative, names subsystem + change.
225	- **Description**: PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE WITH `path/file.go:LINE` + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE.
226	- **AC**: numbered, each verifiable; at least one names a `Test*` function or `go test -run` filter; final two lines are `cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`.
227	- **Labels**: `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention) when applicable.
228	- **Parent + Deps**: explicit. State "no deps" if there are none.
229	- **Self-test**: re-read the bead cold. If a competent agent given only the bead body cannot pick a file to edit and run tests without asking, retrofit before dispatch.
230	
231	Do not cite `/tmp/...` plan files as load-bearing context — they do not survive between machines or sessions. Inline the relevant excerpt into the description instead.
232	
233	<!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:START -->
234	<!-- Source: claude/system-prompts/focused.md -->
235	# System Instructions
236	
237	**Execute ONLY what is requested:**
238	
239	- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
240	- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
241	- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.
242	
243	**Response Style:**
244	- Be concise and direct
245	- Skip preamble and postamble
246	- Provide complete information without unnecessary elaboration
247	- Stop immediately when the task is done
248	
249	**When coding:**
250	- Write only code needed to pass tests
251	- No gold-plating or speculative features
252	- Follow existing patterns and conventions
253	- Add only requested functionality
254	<!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:END -->
255	# important-instruction-reminders
256	Do what has been asked; nothing more, nothing less.
257	NEVER create files unless they're absolutely necessary for achieving your goal.
258	ALWAYS prefer editing an existing file to creating a new one.
259	NEVER proactively create documentation files (*.md) or README files unless the task explicitly requires creating or editing documentation.
260

data.text: Now let me find the "Testing and Quality" section in CLAUDE.md:

data.output:
117	## Testing and Quality
118	
119	**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:
120	
121	```bash
122	# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
123	cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
124	```
125	
126	Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.
127	
128	- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
129	- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
130	- Code formatting with `go fmt`
131	- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained
132	
133	### Pre-commit Checks
134	
135	The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:
136	
137	```bash
138	# Run all pre-commit checks
139	lefthook run pre-commit
140	
141	# Or stage files and run checks
142	git add <files>
143	lefthook run pre-commit
144	```
145	
146	### Test fixtures
147	
148	Tests and demos that need a clean ddx-initialized git repo (without polluting this project) should use the shared fixture helper rather than rolling their own `t.TempDir()` setup. Call `testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, profile)` (under `cli/internal/testutils/`) which wraps `scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh` and auto-cleans via `t.Cleanup`. Profiles: `minimal`, `standard`, `multi-project`, `federated`. See `scripts/build-fixture-repo.md` for layout details.
149	
150	Pre-commit checks include:
151	- Secrets detection
152	- Binary file prevention
153	- Debug statement detection
154	- Merge conflict detection
155	- DDx configuration validation
156	- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing
157	
158	## CLI Command Overview
159	
160	The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:
161

data.text: Let me check the lefthook config to understand what checks run:

data.output:
1	# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
2	# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
3	# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook
4	
5	# Configuration
6	colors: true
7	no_tty: false
8	assertion: false
9	
10	# Reusable command groups
11	command_groups:
12	  go_checks: &go_checks
13	    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
14	    tags: [go, backend]
15	
16	  security_checks: &security_checks
17	    tags: [security]
18	
19	  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
20	    tags: [format, style]
21	
22	# ====================
23	# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
24	# ====================
25	pre-commit:
26	  parallel: true
27	
28	  commands:
29	    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
30	    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
31	    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
32	    conflicts:
33	      name: "Check merge conflicts"
34	      run: |
35	        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
36	        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
37	        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
38	          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
39	          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
40	        done | head -1)
41	        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
42	          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
43	          exit 1
44	        fi
45	        exit 0
46	      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."
47	
48	    git-config-health:
49	      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
50	      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."
51	
52	    beads-tracker-health:
53	      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
54	      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."
55	
56	    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
57	    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
58	    # debug-go:
59	    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
60	    #   glob: "*.go"
61	    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
62	    #   run: |
63	    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
64	    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
65	    #     else
66	    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
67	    #     fi
68	    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"
69	
70	    debug-python:
71	      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
72	      glob: "*.py"
73	      run: |
74	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
75	          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
76	        else
77	          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
78	        fi
79	      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"
80	
81	    # Go formatting - native gofmt
82	    go-fmt:
83	      <<: *go_checks
84	      name: "Go Format"
85	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
86	      run: |
87	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
88	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
89	          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
90	          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
91	          exit 1
92	        fi
93	      skip:
94	        - merge
95	        - rebase
96	
97	    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
98	    go-lint:
99	      <<: *go_checks
100	      name: "Go Lint"
101	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
102	      run: |
103	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
104	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
105	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
106	          exit 1
107	        fi
108	        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
109	        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
110	        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
111	        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
112	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
113	        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
114	        for pkg in $packages; do
115	          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
116	        done
117	      skip:
118	        - merge
119	        - rebase
120	
121	    # Go build - only for changed packages
122	    go-build:
123	      <<: *go_checks
124	      name: "Go Build"
125	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
126	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
127	      skip:
128	        - merge
129	        - rebase
130	
131	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
132	    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
133	    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
134	    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
135	    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
136	    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
137	    evidence-lint:
138	      <<: *go_checks
139	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
140	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
141	      run: |
142	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
143	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
144	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
145	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
146	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
147	        violations=""
148	        for f in $staged; do
149	          bare="${f#cli/}"
150	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
151	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
152	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
153	          fi
154	        done
155	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
156	          printf '%s' "$violations"
157	          exit 1
158	        fi
159	        exit 0
160	      skip:
161	        - merge
162	        - rebase
163	      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."
164	
165	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
166	    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
167	    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
168	    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
169	    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
170	    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
171	    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
172	    runtime-lint:
173	      <<: *go_checks
174	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
175	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
176	      run: |
177	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
178	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
179	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
180	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
181	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
182	        violations=""
183	        for f in $staged; do
184	          bare="${f#cli/}"
185	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
186	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
187	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
188	          fi
189	        done
190	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
191	          printf '%s' "$violations"
192	          exit 1
193	        fi
194	        exit 0
195	      skip:
196	        - merge
197	        - rebase
198	      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."
199	
200	    # Go tests - targeted testing
201	    go-test:
202	      <<: *go_checks
203	      name: "Go Tests"
204	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
205	      run: |
206	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
207	        for pkg in $packages; do
208	          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
209	            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
210	          fi
211	        done
212	      skip:
213	        - merge
214	        - rebase
215	      env:
216	        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
217	        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"
218	
219	    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
220	    secrets:
221	      <<: *security_checks
222	      name: "Scan for secrets"
223	      run: |
224	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
225	          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
226	        else
227	          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
228	          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
229	          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')
230	
231	          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
232	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
233	            echo "Potential secrets detected"
234	            exit 1
235	          fi
236	
237	          # AWS keys
238	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
239	            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
240	            exit 1
241	          fi
242	
243	          # GitHub tokens
244	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
245	            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
246	            exit 1
247	          fi
248	        fi
249	      exclude:
250	        - "*.md"
251	        - "go.sum"
252	        - "package-lock.json"
253	        - "*_test.go"
254	        - "**/dist/**"
255	        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
256	      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"
257	
258	    # Binary and large file check
259	    large-files:
260	      name: "Check file sizes"
261	      run: |
262	        for file in {staged_files}; do
263	          size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
264	          if [ "$size" -gt 5242880 ]; then
265	            echo "Large file detected: $file ($(($size / 1048576))MB)"
266	            exit 1
267	          fi
268	        done
269	      exclude:
270	        - "*.png"
271	        - "*.jpg"
272	        - "*.jpeg"
273	        - "*.gif"
274	        - "*.svg"
275	        - "*.ico"
276	        - "*.pdf"
277	        - "*.woff"
278	        - "*.woff2"
279	        - "*.ttf"
280	        - "*.eot"
281	        # bead store grows monotonically with queue activity; expected to exceed 5MB
282	        - ".ddx/beads.jsonl"
283	        # archive partner accumulates closed beads forever; trends past 5MB
284	        - ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"
285	      fail_text: "Large files detected. Use Git LFS for files over 5MB"
286	
287	    # DDx validation - only when relevant files change
288	    ddx-validate:
289	      name: "Validate DDx"
290	      run: |
291	        # Only run if DDx files are staged
292	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(\.ddx\.yml|templates/|patterns/|prompts/)'; then
293	          exit 0
294	        fi
295	
296	        # Strip git's local-env-vars before invoking ddx so any git commands
297	        # the ddx process spawns honor cmd.Dir / -C <dir> rather than the
298	        # GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc. inherited from this hook's context.
299	        # Without this, ddx's git invocations mutate THIS repo (the hook's),
300	        # producing core.bare=true and stray core.worktree corruption.
301	        # ddx-64ac553a Phase 0 (defensive triage; real fix is the central
302	        # git.Command wrapper in Phase 2a).
303	        unset $(git rev-parse --local-env-vars)
304	
305	        # Check for DDx command availability
306	        if command -v ddx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
307	          ddx doctor >/dev/null 2>&1
308	        elif [ -x "./cli/ddx" ]; then
309	          ./cli/ddx doctor >/dev/null 2>&1
310	        fi
311	      fail_text: "DDx validation failed. Run 'ddx doctor' for details"
312	
313	    skill-schema:
314	      <<: *go_checks
315	      name: "Validate skill metadata"
316	      run: |
317	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^(skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md|cli/internal/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md)$'; then
318	          exit 0
319	        fi
320	        make skill-schema
321	      skip:
322	        - merge
323	        - rebase
324	
325	    sync-embedded-skills:
326	      name: "Sync cli/internal/skills/ddx from top-level skills/ddx"
327	      root: "cli/"
328	      run: |
329	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^skills/ddx/'; then
330	          exit 0
331	        fi
332	        make copy-skills
333	        cd .. && git add cli/internal/skills/ddx/
334	      stage_fixed: true
335	      skip:
336	        - merge
337	        - rebase
338	      fail_text: "Failed to sync cli/internal/skills/ddx from skills/ddx. Run 'cd cli && make copy-skills' manually and stage the result."
339	
340	    design-md-lint:
341	      name: "DESIGN.md lint"
342	      glob: ".stitch/DESIGN.md"
343	      run: |
344	        if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
345	          echo "bun not found; skipping DESIGN.md lint"
346	          exit 0
347	        fi
348	        bun install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || bun install
349	        bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
350	      fail_text: "DESIGN.md lint failed. Run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md"
351	
352	    test-engineer-persona-drift:
353	      name: "Guard test-engineer persona (ddx-482fad2f)"
354	      glob: "library/personas/test-engineer.md"
355	      run: |
356	        if grep -q "Mock all external dependencies" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
357	          echo "ERROR: 'Mock all external dependencies' is forbidden in the test-engineer persona (see ddx-482fad2f)"
358	          exit 1
359	        fi
360	        if grep -q "test doubles (mocks" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
361	          echo "ERROR: 'test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)' lists mocks and stubs as equivalent — forbidden (see ddx-482fad2f)"
362	          exit 1
363	        fi
364	      fail_text: "test-engineer persona contains forbidden mock-promotion phrases"
365	
366	# ====================
367	# CI/CD - Full validation (all files)
368	# ====================
369	ci:
370	  parallel: true
371	
372	  commands:
373	    # Full conflict check
374	    conflicts-all:
375	      name: "Check all merge conflicts"
376	      run: |
377	        # Check for actual merge conflict markers (exactly 7 characters followed by space or newline)
378	        conflicts=$(find . -type f ! -path "./.git/*" ! -path "./cli/.git/*" ! -name "lefthook.yml" \
379	          ! -path "*/node_modules/*" ! -path "*/.svelte-kit/*" ! -path "./cli/internal/server/frontend/build/*" \
380	          ! -path "./website/public/*" ! -path "./cli/build/*" \
381	          -exec grep -l "^<<<<<<<[ \t]\|^=======\$\|^>>>>>>>[ \t]\|^||||||[ \t]" {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -1)
382	        if [ -n "$conflicts" ]; then
383	          echo "Merge conflict markers found in: $conflicts"
384	          exit 1
385	        fi
386	
387	    # Full secrets scan
388	    secrets-all:
389	      <<: *security_checks
390	      name: "Full secrets scan"
391	      run: |
392	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
393	          gitleaks detect --verbose --redact
394	        else
395	          echo "Consider installing gitleaks for comprehensive secret scanning"
396	        fi
397	
398	    # Go full test suite
399	    go-test-all:
400	      <<: *go_checks
401	      name: "Full Go test suite"
402	      root: "cli/"
403	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
404	
405	    bead-schema:
406	      <<: *go_checks
407	      name: "Bead Schema Guard"
408	      run: 'make bead-schema'
409	
410	    skill-schema:
411	      <<: *go_checks
412	      name: "Skill Metadata Guard"
413	      run: 'make skill-schema'
414	
415	    # Go formatting check all files
416	    go-fmt-all:
417	      <<: *go_checks
418	      name: "Check all Go formatting"
419	      root: "cli/"
420	      run: |
421	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
422	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
423	          echo "Unformatted files: $unformatted"
424	          exit 1
425	        fi
426	
427	    # Go lint all files
428	    go-lint-all:
429	      <<: *go_checks
430	      name: "Lint all Go files"
431	      root: "cli/"
432	      run: |
433	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
434	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
435	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
436	          exit 1
437	        fi
438	        golangci-lint run --timeout=5m
439	
440	    # Go build all targets
441	    go-build-all:
442	      <<: *go_checks
443	      name: "Build all targets"
444	      root: "cli/"
445	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
446	
447	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage. Repo-wide
448	    # invariant check; runs in CI even when no Go files were staged
449	    # in the triggering commit so historical regressions still surface.
450	    evidence-lint-all:
451	      <<: *go_checks
452	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts) — full tree"
453	      root: "cli/"
454	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...'
455	
456	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: full-tree gate. The pre-commit hook
457	    # only checks staged-package diagnostics; this CI gate enforces the
458	    # post-cleanup invariant — zero forbidden runtime fields and zero
459	    # legacy *Options types anywhere in cli/. Activated now that the
460	    # RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions/ExecuteBeadOptions
461	    # retirement beads have all landed (ddx-ade5ebb3, ddx-a8eaef0a).
462	    runtime-lint-all:
463	      <<: *go_checks
464	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options) — full tree"
465	      root: "cli/"
466	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...'
467	
468	    # FEAT-006 routing-lint: dead-code / anti-reintroduction gate for
469	    # the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by ddx-3bd7396a
470	    # (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9). Fails
471	    # CI if any of ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef,
472	    # AdaptiveMinTier, workersByHarness, or the retired --escalate /
473	    # --override-model / agent.routing.profile_ladders /
474	    # agent.routing.model_overrides flag/config tokens reappear in
475	    # cli/ Go source.
476	    routing-lint-all:
477	      <<: *go_checks
478	      name: "Routing Lint (FEAT-006 compensating-routing helpers retired) — full tree"
479	      root: "cli/"
480	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...'
481	
482	    docs-routing-lint:
483	      name: "Docs Routing Lint (legacy DDx routing surfaces allowlisted)"
484	      run: 'bash scripts/check-legacy-routing-docs.sh'
485	
486	    # YAML validation
487	    yaml-validate:
488	      name: "Validate YAML files"
489	      glob: "**/*.{yml,yaml}"
490	      run: |
491	        if command -v yq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
492	          for file in {all_files}; do
493	            yq eval . "$file" >/dev/null || exit 1
494	          done
495	        fi
496	
497	# ====================
498	# Pre-push hooks - Extended validation
499	# ====================
500	pre-push:
501	  parallel: false
502	
503	  commands:
504	    git-config-health:
505	      name: "Git config health"
506	      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
507	      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before pushing."
508	      tags: [policy]
509	
510	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
511	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
512	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
513	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
514	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
515	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
516	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
517	    merge-policy:
518	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
519	      run: |
520	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
521	        remote="${1:-origin}"
522	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
523	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
524	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
525	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
526	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
527	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
528	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
529	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
530	          exit 0
531	        fi
532	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
533	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
534	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
535	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
536	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
537	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
538	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
539	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
540	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
541	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
542	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
543	          echo ""
544	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
545	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
546	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
547	          exit 1
548	        fi
549	        exit 0
550	      tags: [policy]
551	
552	    # Run full test suite before push
553	    full-tests:
554	      name: "Full test suite"
555	      root: "cli/"
556	      run: |
557	        # Git hooks export local repository variables such as GIT_DIR. The
558	        # test suite creates nested fixture repositories, so scrub those vars
559	        # before tests spawn git.
560	        for var in $(git rev-parse --local-env-vars); do
561	          unset "$var"
562	        done
563	
564	        # Race detector requires cgo. Explicit CGO_ENABLED=1 overrides any
565	        # inherited CGO_ENABLED=0 in the shell environment.
566	        export CGO_ENABLED=1
567	        if ! (command -v gcc >/dev/null 2>&1 || command -v cc >/dev/null 2>&1 || command -v clang >/dev/null 2>&1); then
568	          echo "ERROR: go test -race requires CGO_ENABLED=1 and a C compiler (gcc/cc/clang)."
569	          echo "Install a C compiler (e.g. apt install gcc or brew install gcc) to use the race-detector gate."
570	          exit 1
571	        fi
572	        go test -race -cover ./...
573	      tags: [go, test]
574	
575	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
576	    no-debug:
577	      name: "No debug statements"
578	      run: |
579	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
580	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
581	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
582	          status=$?
583	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
584	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
585	            exit 0
586	          fi
587	          exit 1
588	        fi
589	
590	# Skip configuration
591	# Usage: LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE=go-test,secrets git commit
592	# Or: LEFTHOOK=0 git commit (skip all hooks)
593	
594	# Install hooks: lefthook install
595	# Run manually: lefthook run pre-commit
596	# Run CI checks: lefthook run ci
597

data.text:
Now I have all the context I need. Let me implement the three changes:

1. `cli/tools/test-timing/main.go` — JSON parser for top-N slowest tests
2. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — new `test-timing` job
3. `CLAUDE.md` — document the convention

data.output: File created successfully at: /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-626f3120-20260509T223123-0f2ff4e4/cli/tools/test-timing/main.go (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

data.text: Now add the `test-timing` job to the CI workflow:

input.old_string:
# Perf tests — non-gating; run weekly on schedule or on manual dispatch only
  perf:
input.new_string:
# Test timing — runs the full suite with -json output and uploads durable timing artifacts.
  # Non-gating: timing data is captured regardless of ci job outcome.
  test-timing:
    name: Test Timing
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: cli
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum

    - name: Run tests with JSON output
      run: go test -json -race -cover ./... 2>&1 | tee ../test-output.json || true

    - name: Generate timing summary
      if: always()
      run: go run ./tools/test-timing < ../test-output.json > ../test-timing-summary.md

    - name: Upload test timing artifacts
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: test-timing-${{ github.run_id }}
        path: |
          test-output.json
          test-timing-summary.md
        retention-days: 30

  # Perf tests — non-gating; run weekly on schedule or on manual dispatch only
  perf:

data.output: The file /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-626f3120-20260509T223123-0f2ff4e4/.github/workflows/ci.yml has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

data.text: Now document the convention in CLAUDE.md, after the "Pre-commit Checks" section and before "Test fixtures":

input.new_string:
### Test Timing Artifacts

CI uploads test timing as a JSON artifact. The `test-timing` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `go test -json -race -cover ./...` and uploads two artifacts (retained 30 days) to the GitHub Actions run page:

- `test-timing-<run-id>`: raw `go test -json` output for post-processing
- `test-timing-summary.md`: Markdown table of the top 20 slowest tests (generated by `cli/tools/test-timing/`)

Use these to investigate slow-test regressions — download from the Actions run page after a push or PR.

### Test fixtures

data.output: The file /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-626f3120-20260509T223123-0f2ff4e4/CLAUDE.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

ci: emit go test -json across the test suite and upload as artifact for timing visibility [ddx-626f3120]

)"

data.text:
Commit is clean. All ACs satisfied:

1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` has a new `test-timing` job that runs `go test -json -race -cover ./...` and uploads the JSON output as a GitHub Actions artifact.
2. Retention is 30 days (`retention-days: 30`).
3. `cli/tools/test-timing/main.go` parses the JSON and emits a top-N Markdown summary, also uploaded as an artifact.
4. `CLAUDE.md` documents the convention under "Testing and Quality → Test Timing Artifacts".
5. The CI job is fully configured and will run on every push/PR.
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` green (all packages pass).
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
